---
title: "Update Checklist"
---

Follow these steps in order at each annual update cycle.

## Raw data downloads

1. Download the new OECD Global Revenue Statistics CSV. Verify the filename and column structure against the previous vintage before saving to `raw_data/`.

2. Download the new IMF GFS time series CSV. Navigate to Government Finance Statistics, Revenue, Statement of Operations, and download all components. Verify column structure.

3. Refresh the WID raw data download. Uncomment the download block in `2_1_factorshares_importwid_2026.do`, verify the API indicator codes, and re-run. Update `wid_rename_2026.do` and `wid_labels_2026.do` if WID has changed any variable names.

4. Download the new ICTD Government Revenue Dataset. Verify the sheet name and column layout against the previous vintage.

5. Download the new UN SNA CSV files by institutional sector. Add new numbered files following the pattern in `2_3_factorshares_importsna2024_1import.do`. Note: this source is complementary to the WID database and in 2026 was not updated for the majority of countries.

6. Refresh the OECD SNA data for China (`CHN_raw_oecdsna_2026.csv`). Download from OECD Data Explorer, Annual non-financial accounts by institutional sector.

7. Update the IMF WEO GDP file (`gdp_weo_new.dta`) to cover the latest years used by the ICTD conversion step.

8. Update the INSEE tax credits file for France if new years are available.

## Re-run and verify

1. Run `1_01_revenue_crosswalks.do`. No changes expected unless new tax categories have appeared in the source data.

2. Run `1_1_revenue_oecd.do`. Check the backfill country list and review the four powercode corrections (ROU, HRV, UKR, BGR).

3. Run `1_3_revenue_imf.do`. Check for new columns in the raw CSV if the IMF extended the time series. Review any unmatched country names after the ISO3 merge.

4. Run `1_4_revenue_ns_3_new_updt.do` if any of the 16 active NS countries have new data. Update the corresponding raw file in `raw_data/NS/3_new` first.

5. Run `1_5_revenue_ictd.do`. Verify the Belarus 1994 GDP correction and the ICTD no-GDP country list.

6. Run the full revenue pipeline through `1_12_revenue_clean.do`.

7. Run the full factor shares pipeline through `2_8_factorshares_clean.do`. Run the comparison block at the end of `2_8` manually before proceeding to the ETR module.

8. Run `3_1_ETR_revenue_source.do`. For any country with new source data or extended years, add or revise the relevant country block. Update the output folder path if the vintage label changes.

9. Run `3_2` through `3_5`. Review the diagnostic tabulations at the top of `3_6_ETR_countrychecks.do` before proceeding to winsorization.

10. Run `3_6` and `3_7`. Activate the WDD denominator block in `3_7` manually if CIT and NDP values in USD billions are needed for the Atlas website.

## Quality checks

1. Check the edition comparison table against the previous vintage. Flag ETR differences above 5 percentage points for review.

2. Verify that the country count in each region is stable across the full time series.

3. Update the data vintage table in the [Data Sources](data-sources.qmd) page.

4. Update the dofile block in the relevant inventory page for any file that was modified during this update cycle.
